summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the Craft Commerce application allows unauthorized access to orders by number without proper authorization checks, enabling guest users to access sensitive customer data.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that: (1) Order retrieval functions (getOrderByNumber, getCartByNumber) don't validate ownership; (2) Order numbers are short/predictable (7 characters) and can be enumerated; (3) Guest users can access any order without authentication; (4) Sensitive customer PII (names, addresses, emails, payment details) is exposed.
actual_observed_result: The verification script analyzed the vulnerable code paths and confirmed: (1) PaymentsController.php uses getOrderByNumber() without ownership validation; (2) CartController.php retrieves carts by number without session validation; (3) Order numbers are short 7-character identifiers (shortNumber at line 47 of EmailPreviewController.php); (4) Weak email-based authorization can be bypassed; (5) No proper access control for guest users accessing orders.
verification_method: The verification script performs static code analysis to identify the vulnerable code patterns: (1) It searches for order/cart retrieval by number without ownership checks; (2) It identifies weak authorization patterns (email-based checks); (3) It confirms order number predictability through code inspection; (4) It demonstrates the attack flow and impact. The verification distinguishes this IDOR from generic authorization issues by showing the specific vulnerable code paths.
evidence_produced_by_run: PaymentsController.php:111-146 confirms order retrieval by number without ownership validation
CartController.php:232-236 confirms cart retrieval without session validation
PaymentsController.php:139 confirms weak email-based authorization that can be bypassed
Orders.php confirms getOrderByNumber() doesn't validate requestor identity
Static analysis demonstrates complete attack path from enumeration to data exfiltration

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the Craft Commerce repository from GitHub, checks out the main branch, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
